tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function is impaired (elevated creatinine, significant proteinuria, low urine specific gravity), suggesting possible chronic kidney disease. Urinalysis shows glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, and abnormal urine appearance, but no evidence of infection. Blood glucose and electrolytes are within normal limits, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low, possibly due to anemia or altered red cell turnover. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying causes of proteinuria and renal impairment.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
